articles.title: China’s Economy Starts to Show Cracks From Iran War
articles.summary: China’s strategic reserves of oil and natural gas have insulated it somewhat, but its manufacturing-based economy is beginning to falter.

articles.title: How China is responding to the stalling of talks between the U.S. and Iran
articles.summary: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East

articles.title: Top Iranian Official in Russia for Talks With Putin on Middle East War
articles.summary: Abbas Araghchi, Iran’s foreign minister, will meet with President Vladimir V. Putin for “necessary coordination,” as negotiations with the U.S. to end the conflict have stalled.

articles.title: Inside the Chaos at the Correspondents’ Dinner, and How the U.S. Mint Is Buying Drug Cartel Gold
articles.summary: Plus, marathon runners shatter a world record.

articles.title: Even Before the Gunfire. Many Guests Bore the Scars of Political Violence
articles.summary: Gunfire at a press gala on Saturday underscored the ever growing club of political figures whose lives have been upended by violence.

articles.title: Grievance Propelled Gala Attack Suspect Across Country, Authorities Say
articles.summary: A man who has worked as a tutor and graduated from the California Institute of Technology is being held by authorities in connection with the armed attack at the White House correspondents’ dinner.

articles.title: The U.S. Started the War. The Rest of the World Is Feeling the Effects.
articles.summary: In just eight weeks, much of the global economy has been knocked sideways. America has mostly been spared from the tumult.

articles.title: Inflation is sucking the life out of teacher pay raises, report says
articles.summary: A new review of state education data shows teacher pay increases can't keep up with inflation and fewer students are enrolled in public schools.

articles.title: Climate disaster victims are rebuilding using prefab homes from boxy to bespoke
articles.summary: Wildfires, hurricanes, tornadoes and floods fueled by manmade climate change are changing the housing industry. That's because people seeking to rebuild in disaster-prone regions are turning away from

articles.title: Alleged White House Correspondents' Dinner shooter set to appear in federal court
articles.summary: The suspected gunman will face charges, including assault on a federal officer using a dangerous weapon.

articles.title: Scientists search the microbiome for clues to the rise in colorectal cancers
articles.summary: Unlike many cancers, colorectal cancer has become more lethal for people at younger ages. Doctors are sleuthing out why.

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman: Tech CEOs head to court Monday over fate of OpenAI
articles.summary: The former OpenAI business partners are embroiled in a high-stakes dispute over the future of one of the world's top AI companies.

articles.title: Ingenious? Orwellian? Or both? Supreme Court considers constitutionality of 'geofence' warrants
articles.summary: The technique allows police to tap into giant tech-firm databases to find out who was near the scene of a crime and may have been involved.

articles.title: Oil Rises and Stocks Waver as Peace Talks Stall
articles.summary: Oil prices rose and stocks fell after President Trump called off a trip to Pakistan by two U.S. negotiators for peace talks with Iran.

articles.title: Palestinian officials hail local elections in a Gaza community and the West Bank
articles.summary: Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.

articles.title: Monte Coleman, who won 3 Super Bowls as a Washington linebacker, dies at age 68
articles.summary: The hard-nosed linebacker, who won three Super Bowls and later coached Arkansas-Pine Bluff, has died. UAPB and the Commanders announced his death Sunday. No cause of death was disclosed.

articles.title: ‘Shots Fired!’: Inside the Pandemonium at the Washington Hilton
articles.summary: Guests dived to the floor and took cover as Secret Service agents climbed over tables to protect some of the country’s most high-ranking officials, including President Trump.

articles.title: Egypt's Grand Museum opens after many delays
articles.summary: We visit the new Grand Egyptian Museum, a spacious museum outside Cairo dedicated to displaying artifacts from ancient Egypt.  It has finally opened its doors after decades of planning and constructio

articles.title: Trump doubts shooter motivated by Iran war as peace talks on hold
articles.summary: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.

articles.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
articles.summary: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.

articles.title: In a rare interview, a leader of the world's largest right-wing group talks to NPR
articles.summary: The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.

articles.title: Mali reeling after coordinated attacks hit multiple cities
articles.summary: Gunfire and explosions rocked Mali's capital and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups exploited worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.

articles.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
articles.summary: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
